meta.updated: Updated May 29, 2026 (from May QBR & weekly calls)
meta.changelog: Recent changes (May 2026): Orita retained · Maverick not renewing after pilot · Mercana moved to active (credit pricing) · Retention.com confirmed discontinued · Rebuy added (smart cart) · Arcady & Recart added as pending · Sensit added under evaluation · Post Pilot: new DM-triggered flows, but paused (budget).

items.name: Retention.com
items.role:
Anonymous visitor ID
Turned off — segment archived
items.what: Identified anonymous website visitors and matched them to known email profiles for outreach. No longer in use.
connections: Previously pushed identified visitor data into Klaviyo profiles
items.notes: May 26 QBR: Joey confirmed Retention.com was turned off last year — analysis showed it added very few people to the list. Alex is archiving the leftover Retention.com segments still sitting in Klaviyo (archiving, not deleting). Kept on the diagram for history; no longer active.

items.name: Digioh
items.what: Powers all opt-in popups, exit-intent forms, and the product recommendation quiz on alen.com. Replaced Amped (acquired by Mailchimp). Better at targeting anonymous visitors than alternatives.
connections: Form submissions subscribe to lists via "integration pipelines"
Planned: webhook to trigger SMS opt-in after email capture (not yet launched)
items.notes: Winston asked (Apr 9) whether Digioh is still needed since Postscript rebuilt popups. Erik’s position: keep it for quiz + anonymous targeting. Head-to-head test was planned but never executed before his departure. Integration uses "pipelines" — each form maps to a Klaviyo list. 8+ active forms plus UTM-targeted variants.

items.name: Postscript
items.what: SMS platform — DATA-IN side. Runs its own SMS opt-in pop-ups/forms that collect subscribers (it took over SMS pop-ups, which overlaps with Digioh — clarify which forms live where).
connections: Planned: webhook for combined email+SMS opt-in (not yet live)
items.notes: See the outbound "Postscript" card for the full SMS-marketing notes. Note: Postscript has taken over SMS pop-ups — clarify overlap with Digioh forms.

items.name: Segment Ninja
items.what: Appends Claritas PRIZM household data to Klaviyo profiles — home value, household size, income, lifestyle clusters (Country Squires, Network Neighbors, Middleburg Managers). Household-level, not individual.
connections: Builds prospecting segments for direct mail
PRIZM data for ad retargeting (Joey wants 70/30 retarget/prospect split)
items.notes: ~$165 + 155k profile credits through end of 2026. Erik was running backfill of all single-purifier purchasers. Target: 4+ bedrooms, 2,500+ sqft = multi-purifier candidates. This data feeds Post Pilot prospecting lists for the May 21-29 annual sale.

items.name: Mercana
items.what: AI-powered individual customer enrichment — home value (Redfin), bedrooms, occupation, interests, social. Much more granular than Claritas.
connections: Complements (not replaces) PRIZM household data — individual-level layer
items.owner: Daniel & Vijay at Mercana. Credit-based pricing (enrichment credits).
items.notes: May 29 Mercana sync: moving forward and being integrated into the segmentation strategy. Alex weaves Mercana segments into June/July campaigns and into the highest-producing flows first. Pricing is credit-based, so be mindful of enrichment volume. Key finding: many healthcare professionals → B2B angle. Horacio also exploring Mercana for postcard design.

items.name: Fairing
items.what: Post-purchase attribution surveys ("How did you hear about us?"). Response data feeds into Klaviyo profiles for segmentation.
items.notes: Listed in original tech stack integration list.

items.name: Triple Whale
items.what: Analytics and attribution platform. Sonar feature enriches Klaviyo with event-level attribution data.
items.notes: Sonar integration feeds event data into Klaviyo.

items.name: Shopify Plus
items.role: E-commerce storefront. Orders, products, customers, checkout.
items.what: The store. All product data, orders, customer records, and checkout flow live here. Shopify Plus tier enables checkout extensibility (SMS consent collection, Shopify Scripts).
items.owner: Alen in-house. Improving (dev team) handles code changes.
items.notes: Source of truth for all order/product data. Always coordinate with the Improving team for any storefront code changes (theme updates, reward tiers, etc.).

items.name: Recharge
items.what: Manages filter replacement subscriptions. Subscription renewal orders flow into Klaviyo, which is why campaign revenue can look inflated if you don’t isolate checkout-only revenue.
items.notes: This is why Erik created the "checkout-only revenue" custom metric in Klaviyo. Without it, standard revenue metrics include auto-renewals, making campaign attribution unreliable.

items.name: Yotpo
items.what: Loyalty program AND product/site reviews. 600 pts = free Sentra starter kit ($30 value). 200 pts = free shipping. Points stack with sale discounts (Joey confirmed Apr 17).
connections: Loyalty events trigger flows; some Yotpo emails to be moved into Klaviyo
items.owner: CSM: Nicole (Miguel rolled off). Improving implements reward tiers.
items.notes: UPDATE (May calls): Yotpo currently sends its OWN loyalty/review emails — Alex wants an audit to move some sends into Klaviyo for more control + cleaner metrics. Open question: confirm what review collection is handled by Yotpo vs Klaviyo. 600-pt Sentra + 200-pt free-shipping rewards live.

items.name: Shogun
items.what: Landing page and content page builder for the Shopify store.
items.notes: Used for landing pages. Low priority for ramp-up.

items.name: PickyStory
items.what: Product bundling and upsell tool on the Shopify store.
items.notes: Low priority for ramp-up.

items.name: Affirm
items.what: BNPL at checkout. Integrated through Shopify.
items.notes: Unclear if direct Klaviyo integration is needed — already working through Shopify.

items.name: Rebuy
items.what: Smart-cart and product-upsell engine running in the cart and checkout experience.
items.owner: Alen in-house (Winston). Improving supports site code.
items.notes: Added after the May QBR — already live but missing here. Operates on-site, not on the retention side. Winston wants more post-purchase with it. Watch for whether any Rebuy data can feed Klaviyo cross-sell logic.

items.name: Recart
items.role:
Cart/SMS recovery
Under review — may overlap
items.what: Cart-abandonment / SMS recovery tool. Surfaced on an implementation list during the May QBR. Function overlaps with tools already in the stack.
connections: Would run on cart / checkout (if kept)
items.notes: May 26 QBR: Winston said it "should be taken out"; Alex noted it is similar to Rebuy and Postscript, so it likely overlaps. Decision needed — confirm whether any active Recart implementation exists. Low priority.

items.name: Klaviyo
items.role:
Email marketing nerve center.
Flows, campaigns, segments, forms, custom metrics.

KEY: Checkout-only revenue metric — isolates real campaign revenue from Recharge renewals.
items.what: The central platform. All email flows, campaigns, segments, embedded forms, and analytics. Connected to nearly every other tool. Houses Erik’s custom "checkout-only revenue" metric, which is critical for accurate attribution.
connections: Receives form submissions via integration pipelines
Receives loyalty events as flow triggers
Receives PRIZM demographic data on profiles
Sends audience data → Orita optimizes who receives each campaign
Shares subscriber data for SMS coordination
Sends segments to trigger postcards; receives postcard-delivery events (pending)
Pushes audience segments for ad retargeting
items.owner: Flowium team. Was Erik → now you.
items.notes: CRITICAL: Find Erik’s checkout-only revenue metric before touching any reports. Standard Klaviyo revenue includes Recharge subscription renewals, which inflates numbers. Joey and Winston expect the isolated metric.

items.name: Zendesk
items.what: Customer support ticketing. Alen recently downsized their CX team.
items.notes: K:BOS recommended Klaviyo Customer Service Hub and AI Customer Agent integrations — could help offset the smaller CX team.

items.name: Orita
items.role:
WHO to send to.
Audience selection.
$1,200/mo — continuing
items.what: AI audience optimization. For each send, determines which subscribers are most likely to engage and suppresses low-propensity recipients. Reduces volume while maintaining or improving revenue.
connections: Reads audience data, optimizes who receives each campaign
Erik suggested connecting for direct mail optimization
items.owner: Ana Lozada-Smith (CSM), Steven Raquet (Agency CSM), Diana Zheng. ~$1,200/mo.
items.notes: DECISION (May 26 QBR): continuing with Orita. Winston: "if I had to pick between Maverick and Orita, I would pick Orita." Values Orita’s ~40k "always-email" segment. Continue using the checkout-only revenue metric to evaluate.

items.name: Maverick
items.role:
WHAT & WHEN to send.
Dropping after pilot ends
items.what: HTML rendering optimization and send-time optimization. Orita picks WHO to send to; Maverick optimizes WHAT they see and WHEN. Official Klaviyo tech partner.
connections: Optimizes email HTML and send timing per recipient
items.notes: DECISION (May 26 QBR): not moving forward after the current agreement ends. Reasons: Winston’s deliverability concern; the 4-way split blocks other A/B tests; judged "not providing as much value" vs Orita. If revisited, must use checkout-only revenue metric.

items.name: Sensit
items.role:
Under evaluation
New tool Alex wants to trial
items.what: New tool that Alex (Alexandra Palau, strategist) wants to trial. Flagged during the May 26 QBR. Specific capability still to be detailed.
items.notes: Surfaced in the May QBR. Action: have Alex share what Sensit does + a demo/URL, then scope a POC and decide. No commercials yet.

items.name: Arcady
items.what: An AI marketing tool Joey flagged as worth evaluating. Recognized / awarded by Klaviyo with a strong client reference list.
items.owner: Winston to get details/URL from Joey; Alex + Carla to evaluate.
items.notes: May QBR + May 21 call: a POC is needed. Action: Winston to pass the specific tool/URL, then Alex/Carla run a POC. No commercials or integration yet.

items.name: Email Campaigns
items.role:
Sent via Klaviyo.
Flows + scheduled campaigns.
items.what: The primary outbound channel. Campaigns and automated flows sent via Klaviyo. Includes lifecycle flows, campaigns, and universal blocks (currently Sentra cross-sell).
items.notes: Joey wants Sentra universal blocks in ALL customer emails for the next 2-3 months. Monthly report goes to Winston before the 3rd Thursday of each month.

items.name: Postscript
items.role:
SMS marketing.
Migrated from Klaviyo SMS
Mar 2026
items.what: SMS platform — OUTBOUND side. SMS flows, campaigns and compliance. Migrated from Klaviyo SMS in March 2026 for better international rates, automations, and compliance tooling.
items.notes: URGENT — Texas exclusion: ALL Texas recipients must be excluded from SMS sends (legal). Verify every active flow and campaign. TCPA compliance checklist requested by Postscript — check status. Postscript-vs-Klaviyo SMS comparison pushed to end of Q2 (last Klaviyo SMS was Mar 6).

items.name: Post Pilot
items.role:
Direct mail postcards.
NEW: delivery triggers Klaviyo flows
Paused — 1 mailing budget left
items.what: Physical postcards triggered by Klaviyo segments. Strategies: retention (reactivate ~47k unsubscribed owners), site retargeting via pixel, cold prospecting via Claritas data. NEW: direct-mail "flows" — postcard-delivery events sent to Klaviyo to trigger flows.
connections: Sends segments to trigger mailings; NEW — postcard-delivery events flow back (integration pending)
Property segments + possible postcard design input
items.owner: Kelly Barden (Post Pilot). Alen side: Horacio (on leave — covered by "another Alexandra").
items.notes: First test mail returned ~9x ROI (Joey: "we stacked the deck"). Post Pilot announced direct-mail FLOWS; data not visible in Klaviyo yet (waiting on integration). BUDGET: only one more mailing approved — Winston wants to PAUSE direct mail and lean Mercana into the email side.

items.name: Meta / Facebook
items.role:
Ad retargeting.
Audience sync from Klaviyo +
PRIZM data from Seg. Ninja
items.what: Ad platform. Veronica manages audience syncs from Klaviyo. Joey wants 70/30 spend split (retargeting vs prospecting) using PRIZM demographic data from Segment Ninja.
connections: Audience sync — engaged, unengaged, unsubscribed lists
items.notes: Erik was coordinating with Veronica on syncing email-unengaged and unsubscribed audiences to Meta for retargeting.
